refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by minimizing data acquisition latency and processing latency.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process is decomposed into two key sub-functions: acquiring timely data from the worksite and processing that data efficiently to update the model.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure latency separately for data acquisition and data processing stages under representative operational scenarios to ensure combined latency meets p95 ≤ 1.0 s.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process and update worksite model with minimal latency

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-bandwidth, low-latency telemetry links for data acquisition
DP2: Implement real-time data processing algorithms optimized for low latency
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1 and DP2 combined to address acquisition and processing latency respectively

potential_sources: Telemetry data acquisition tightly coupled with processing algorithms due to data dependency
coupling_check.notes: The two DPs are logically coupled but can be modularized to minimize diffusion by clear interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes telemetry infrastructure supports required bandwidth and latency
Risk that processing algorithms may not meet latency targets under peak load
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor telemetry performance and processing load; consider fallback or adaptive algorithms if latency targets are not met.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for integrating telemetry data acquisition and processing to update the worksite model

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data streams from vehicles and sensors

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data transmission between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as NGHE Solution integrates data acquisition and processing; low diffusion risk due to centralized implementation.
Limited containment; telemetry data originates here but processing is external, leading to higher diffusion risk if processing logic is duplicated.
Minimal containment; primarily consumes processed data, not responsible for update latency.
Not involved in worksite model update latency; high diffusion risk if incorrectly assigned.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing the update latency functionality within the NGHE Solution module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes too complex
Dependency on telemetry and network link interfaces may introduce latency variability
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure modular internal architecture within NGHE Solution to manage complexity; monitor interface performance.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement clearly decomposes latency requirement into acquisition and processing sub-functions; selected DPs logically address these sub-FRs.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk by centralizing update logic in NGHE Solution
Potential coupling between telemetry data acquisition and processing managed via defined interfaces
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support NGHE Solution as best ownership candidate.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed end-to-end on NGHE Solution module measuring update latency using telemetry and network link interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to NGHE Solution with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor telemetry and network link performance as part of verification
Consider further decomposition if NGHE Solution module complexity grows

decision.justification: Current level of decomposition and assignments satisfy the requirement with clear FR->DP->IM mapping and manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process and update worksite model with minimal latency

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level if further detail is required.

assumptions: Telemetry infrastructure meets latency and bandwidth requirements
Processing algorithms can be optimized to meet latency targets
open_risks: Latency variability due to network conditions
Complexity growth in NGHE Solution module
evidence_needed: Latency performance data from telemetry and network link
Profiling results of processing algorithms under load
